intro: The clearest split is that 1Password uses a subscription model and OpenBao uses a free model. 1Password is Secrets Management tool for developers; OpenBao is open source fork of HashiCorp Vault maintained by the Linux Foundation for secrets management. The data points 1Password at teams with many api keys/tokens and ci/cd pipelines needing secure env vars, and OpenBao at dynamic secrets generation for database credentials in and pki secrets engine for automated tls certificate. Pricing: 1Password is subscription from $2.99; OpenBao is free, free tier. Lock-in is medium for 1Password and high for OpenBao, with DX at 4/5 vs 4/5 and transparency at 5/5 vs 5/5. Only 1Password lists an enterprise tier in the data. Honest trade-off: 1Password is not for solo, static projects; OpenBao is not for shops that rely on vault enterprise features or hcp, so confirm the fit first, then weigh the pivotal factor above against your budget and lock-in tolerance.
quick_take: 1Password is for teams; OpenBao is for teams that want vault functionality under a permissive license; decide based on which pricing model fits your budget.
verdict_a: Choose 1Password if a subscription pricing model fits your procurement and your workload matches teams. Skip it if your context is solo, static projects.
verdict_b: Choose OpenBao if a free pricing model fits your procurement and your workload matches teams that want vault functionality under a permissive license. Skip it if your context is shops that rely on vault enterprise features or hcp.

faq.a: Entry pricing is listed as $2.99 for 1Password and unknown for OpenBao. Both offer a free tier for getting started. Total cost at scale depends on usage and plan details that are not in this dataset, so confirm with each vendor.

faq.q: Can I migrate from 1Password to OpenBao?
faq.a: 1Password is tagged with medium lock-in (items exportable to 1pux/json but deeply integrated workflows). OpenBao is tagged with high lock-in (lf fork of vault, oss). At least one side is high lock-in, so budget migration time for re-implementing integrations.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, 1Password or OpenBao?
faq.a: Both are rated 4/5 for developer experience in this dataset. With equal scores, the choice comes down to the specific SDK surface and docs you will use day to day. Run a short spike on each before deciding.

faq.q: Is OpenBao a good alternative to 1Password?
faq.a: OpenBao is listed as fitting teams that want vault functionality under a permissive license, which overlaps with but is not identical to 1Password's scope. OpenBao is flagged as not for shops that rely on vault enterprise features or hcp, so confirm your context does not fall there.
